person.name: Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy

memoryArtifacts.headline: montmirail  Marie de Montmirail 1248 contre sceau

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Marie de Montmirail (1192-1273), Dame de Meaux , Montmirail and Oisy, La Fere , Saint-Gobain.

«b»History «/b»
Daughter of the Blessed John I st of Montmirail and Helvide Dampierre, it will be the only one offspring.

Married in 1213 to Enguerrand III de Coucy, it will bring to the death of his brother Mathieu de Montmirail in 1262 all the titles and property of the House of Montmirail and Oisy at the house of Coucy .

In 1264, she definitively abandoned all her seigneuries to her son Enguerrand and reserved only the title of Lady of the Fère.

«b»Genealogy «/b»
Marie of Montmirail and Oisy, daughter of Jean I er Montmirail, Lord of Montmirail and Condé wife Enguerrand III; this couple has six children:

1.) Marie de Coucy, who married King Alexander II of Scotland said the Pacific, then to 1251 / 1252 Acre (1296), son of John of Brienne, King of Jerusalem and emperor of the East.

2.) Raoul II of Coucy (250 during the Battle of Mansourah in Egypt during the seventh crusade, near the Earl of Artois, brother of Saint Louis, whom he defended at the price of his blood.

3.) Enguerrand IV of Coucy (1311)

4.) Jean de Coucy

5.) Alix de Coucy who marries the count of Guînes, Arnould III

6.) Jeanne de Coucy who married John I first lord of Mailly, of Ashu and Ploich.

«b»Burial«/b» 
Marie de Montmirail is buried in Longpont Abbey, near her father.

By a charter of 1271, Marie de Montmirail and Oisy, wife of Enguerrand III de Coucy, claimed his burial at Longpont Abbey, near Blessed Jean . Here is this charter:

"1271-I, Mary, Lady of the Fere, Saint-Gobain, to those present will see, hello to our Lord. As for the salvation and the sincerity of my soul, and for the amor and its noble recordance, from which my good father John of his body grows honorably before the moustier of Longpont, he had devoutly and humbly sought it Abbot and at the Convent of Longpont my burial in their house. Seache writes that the Abbot and the Convent say that I have a good will towards them and to their church for the reasons that are said to be of a good, easy and gentle nature, have bestowed upon me my burial, their great thanks in their present church, the convent, the I asked them for my burial in person.

It was made the year of the incarnation of our Lord one thousand two hundred and sixty-one, the Friday after the octaves St. Peter and St. Pol, apostles "

A seal hanging in lakes of Soye: the figure Standing lady who with the left hand holds a bird and of the right a fleur de lys, with the counter-seal of Coucy.

The evidence for Margaret's Guines filiation was first presented in my article, "Eleanor of Castile and the Countess Margaret of Ulster," in "Genealogists' Magazine", 20/10 (June 1982), 335-40 (cols. 669-80). As a daughter of Arnoul de Guines III and Alice de Coucy, Margaret was a 2nd cousin once removed of Queen Eleanor, who almost certainly had a hand in arranging the Guines-Ulster marriage. Through her Coucy descent, Margaret was also a first cousin of Alexander III of Scotland, Edward I's brother-in-law. This Scottish connection had apparently resulted, a year or so before Margaret married Richard de Burgh, in the marriage of her brother Enguerran de Guines to the Scottish heiress Christian de Lindsay, whose issue ultimately became the senior heirs of line to King David I of Scotland.

Margaret was emphatically not a daughter of John de Burgh of Lanvallay, grandson of the infamous justiciar [Hubert] de Burgh. The younger John did leave 3 daughters, two of whose marriages are well attested (neither one to Richard de Burgh nor any member of that family); the third, who was named Margaret, had become a nun before her father died, so the inheritance was divided only between her two elder sisters. The inheritance was never subsequently re-apportioned to provide for the third sister or any of her issue, and she evidently remained in her cloister to die unmarried and childless. [Ref: John Carmi Parsons SGM 3/2/1999-193407]

zh.lifeSummary: 當Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy於大約1200年出生在法國迪瑟 德 米奧時，她的父親Jean de Montmirail36歲，母親Helvide de Dampierre41歲。她於大約1207年與Enguerrand III de Coucy在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1267年9月20日在法國豪-德-福朗斯艾斯隆朋特享年68歲，埋葬在法國皮卡迪艾斯隆朋特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy于大约1200年出生在法国迪瑟 德 米奧时，她的父亲Jean de Montmirail36岁，母亲Helvide de Dampierre41岁。她于大约1207年与Enguerrand III de Coucy在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1267年9月20日在法国豪-德-福朗斯艾斯隆朋特享年68岁，埋葬在法国皮卡迪艾斯隆朋特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: et
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik, Knut.
Swedish: topographic or ornamental name from ek ‘oak’.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farms named Eik, from eik ‘oak’.

variants.name: Eck
meanings: German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name from Middle High German ecke, egge ‘corner’, German Eck(e) (Yiddish ek). This could have been the corner of two streets or, in the case of the German name, the corner of a field or area of land or a rocky outcrop.

German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic compound personal names with the first element agi(n), agil ‘point, edge (of a sword)’, akin to the word in 1 above. This surname (in both senses; see 1 above) is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Swedish and Norwegian: variant of   Ek  .

nameMeanings.name: d'Oisy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Dacey  .

variants.name: Dacey
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Déiseach, a name for a member of the vassal community known as the Déise, a term of uncertain meaning and origin.

variants.name: Dazey
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Daisy  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Marie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Maria . When first introduced to England in the Middle Ages, it was Anglicized in pronunciation and respelled Mary . This French form was reintroduced into the English-speaking world as a separate name in the 19th century, and is still pronounced more or less in the French manner, although sometimes with the stress on the first syllable. It is now often used in combination with other names such as Ellie, Chloe, and Lisa.

en.lifeSummary: When Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy was born about 1200, in Diocese of Meaux, France, her father, Jean de Montmirail, was 36 and her mother, Helvide de Dampierre, was 41. She married Enguerrand III de Coucy about 1207, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died about 20 September 1267, in Longpont, Aisne, Hauts-de-France, France, at the age of 68, and was buried in Longpont, Aisne, Picardie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy est née environ 1200, à Diocèse de Meaux, France, son père, Jean de Montmirail, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Helvide de Dampierre, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé Enguerrand III de Coucy environ 1207, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée environ 20 septembre 1267, à Longpont, Aisne, Hauts-de-France, France, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterrée à Longpont, Aisne, Picardie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy um 1200 in Diocèse de Meaux, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jean de Montmirail 36 und ihre Mutter Helvide de Dampierre 41. Sie hat um 1207 in Scotland Enguerrand III de Coucy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb um 20. September 1267 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Longpont, Aisne, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich und wurde in Longpont, Aisne, Picardie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy è nata il circa 1200 nel Diocèse de Meaux, Francia, sua padre, Jean de Montmirail, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Helvide de Dampierre, aveva 41 anni. Lei sposato Enguerrand III de Coucy circa 1207, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì circa 20 settembre 1267, nel Longpont, Aisne, Hauts-de-France, Francia, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolta a Longpont, Aisne, Picardie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisyが約1200年にフランスディオクeゼ ド メアウックスで生まれた時，父Jean de Montmirailは36歳，母Helvide de Dampierreは41歳であった。約1207年にスコットランドでEnguerrand III de Coucyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1267年9月20日にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスエイスンロングプオントで68歳で死去し，フランスピカーディエイスンロングプオントにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy(이)가 대략 1200년 프랑스 디오ce스 데 모스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jean de Montmirail(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helvide de Dampierre(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 대략 1207년 스컷랜드에서 Enguerrand III de Coucy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1267년 9월 20일 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 아이스니 론그폰트에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 피카디 아이스니 론그폰트에 묻혔다.

mg.about 1200–about 20 September 1267: tany ho any amin'ny 1200–tany ho any amin'ny 20 septambra 1267

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy nasceu aproximadamente 1200, em Diocèse de Meaux, França, seu pai, Jean de Montmirail, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Helvide de Dampierre, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com Enguerrand III de Coucy aproximadamente 1207, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 20 de setembro de 1267, em Longpont, Aisne, Hauts-de-France, França, com 68 anos, e foi sepultada em Longpont, Aisne, Picardie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy родилась около 1200 г. в местности диокeс де мокс, Франция, её отцу по имени Jean de Montmirail было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Helvide de Dampierre, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Enguerrand III de Coucy около 1207 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла около 20 сентября 1267 г. в местности лонгпонт, эисн, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 68 лет и была похоронена в местности лонгпонт, эисн, пикарди, Франция.

sm.about 1200–about 20 September 1267: e tusa o le 1200–e tusa o le 20 Setema 1267

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marie de Montmirail et d'Oisy nació aproximadamente 1200, en Diocèse de Meaux, Francia, su papá, Jean de Montmirail, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Helvide de Dampierre, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Enguerrand III de Coucy aproximadamente 1207, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 20 de septiembre de 1267, en Longpont, Aisne, Hauts-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultada en Longpont, Aisne, Picardie, Francia.
